input:
Sara Hassan
Database Administrator
sara.hassan@email.com |+20 101 234 5678 |/githubgithub.com/sara-hassan |/linkedinlinkedin.com/in/sara-hassan |
sarahassan.com
Education
PDACOE, Gulbarga, Autonomous Institution
B. Tech Computer Science 2010 - 2014, Gulbarga, India
Experience
Database Administrator III On-Site
BNY Mellon International Operations (India) PVT. LTD Aug 2018 - Present, Mumbai, India
•SQL Server Administration:
•Installation and configuration of database servers using slipstream, setting up all maintenance jobs as per standard
policy on standalone as well as cluster environments with latest service packs.
•Installation of SSRS, uploading of .rdls, assigning correct data sources to reports, and granting necessary access to
users & developers on reporting website; aware of SSIS and designing packages.
•Creation and management of logins and users for database applications, assigning permissions, and resolving user
login issues.
•Migration of SQL Server 2005/2008 servers to higher versions.
•Setup of database refresh jobs on QA, DEV , and UAT environments, and fixing orphaned users.
•Troubleshooting performance-related issues.
•Collaborated with developers on multiple projects, providing all required support for testing in QA, UAT & DEV
environments.
•Led Disaster Recovery (DR) tests for the database team.
•Participated in database purge and archive activities.
•Developed codes for automating database administration tasks.
•Worked on automating DR tasks to start agent jobs on multiple servers, and restoring log-shipped databases without
manual intervention post-DR activities.
•Provided support to vendor databases, followed up on vendor calls, and timely escalated to next level when there is
no update in a predefined timeline.
•Installation and configuration of smsql on Windows Server; scheduled jobs for creation and deletion of clones on
SQL Server, and maintained backups using smsql.
•MongoDB Administration:
•Installation and configuration of MongoDB server.
•Creation of databases and collections.
•Creation of new users and granting access using Ops manager.
•Monitoring of database servers using Ops manager.
•Oracle & Sybase Administration:
•Managing and maintaining multiple instances of Databases on Linux and Windows servers.
•Monitoring daily jobs including backups, refresh, and maintenance jobs.
Database Administrator On-Site
Hewlett-Packard India Sales PVT. LTD. (On the payroll of Softenger India PVT. LTD)Sep 2014 - Jul 2018, Bengaluru, India
•Installed SQL Server on standalone as well as Windows cluster environments with latest service packs, including
slipstream installation.
•Installed reporting services.
•Created logins and users, assigning permissions as per requests.
•Performed security audits for all logins, including maintenance

of unused and orphan user logins.
•Created & maintained daily and weekly jobs/maintenance plans, including backup, index rebuild/reorganize, up-
date statistics, and database consistency checks.
•Created linked servers and ensured connectivity between servers.

•Monitored disk space proactively & managed space using data and log file shrinking.
•Monitored blocking, deadlocks, open transactions, and slow running queries during performance issues, highlighting
costly queries to developers.
•Configured alerts for deadlock and blocking to maintain performance.
•Implemented high availability technologies like log shipping, AlwaysOn, mirroring, and its troubleshooting; pos-
sessed knowledge of replication.
•Successfully completed migration of Databases from one server to another.
•Performed DR drills (Online/Offline) on a quarterly basis.
•Utilized PowerShell scripting to monitor, restart SQL service, and get email alerts for service status.
•Maintained TNS entries for Oracle clients as per client requests.
•Interacted with customers for requirements.
•Contacted customers to update the status of handling issues and service requests at every stage of resolution.
•Managed proper escalation and notification matrix for all support levels.
Skills
Operating Systems: Microsoft Windows Server (2003, 2008, 2008 R2, 2012)
Database Management: SQL Server - Sybase ASE Server - Oracle - MongoDB
Database Tools: SSMS - DBArtisan - Studio 3T - SnapShot Manager for SQL Server - MongoDB Ops Manager
High Availability & DR: Log Shipping - AlwaysOn - Mirroring - Replication - DR Drills - smsql
Scripting & Automation: PowerShell Scripting - SQL Automation
Monitoring & Ticketing: HP Service Manager (7.0, 9.0) - SolarWinds DPA - JIRA
Web Servers: IIS 7.0
Reporting & ETL: SSRS - SSIS
Other Technical: TNS Entries
Soft Skills: Customer Interaction - Problem Escalation - Troubleshooting - Performance Tuning

Sara Hassan is a Database Administrator with contact information including email (sara.hassan@email.com), phone number (+20 101 234 5678), and links to her GitHub and LinkedIn profiles, as well as a personal website (sarahassan.com).
Sara Hassan received a B.Tech in Computer Science from PDACOE, Gulbarga, an autonomous institution, between 2010 and 2014 in Gulbarga, India.

From August 2018 to the present, Sara Hassan worked as a Database Administrator III (On-Site) at BNY Mellon International Operations (India) PVT. LTD in Mumbai, India, where she performed SQL Server and MongoDB administration, as well as Oracle & Sybase Administration.

At BNY Mellon, Sara Hassan was responsible for SQL Server administration tasks including installation, configuration, maintenance, user management, migration, troubleshooting, and disaster recovery. She also worked with SSRS, SSIS, and automated database tasks.

From September 2014 to July 2018, Sara Hassan worked as a Database Administrator (On-Site) at Hewlett-Packard India Sales PVT. LTD (on the payroll of Softenger India PVT. LTD) in Bengaluru, India, where she performed SQL Server installation, reporting services installation, user management, and security audits.
The individual managed unused and orphan user logins and maintained daily and weekly database jobs, including backups, index maintenance, statistics updates, and consistency checks.
Linked servers were created and connectivity between servers was ensured. Disk space was proactively monitored and managed using data and log file shrinking.

Performance issues were monitored for blocking, deadlocks, open transactions, and slow queries, with costly queries highlighted to developers. Alerts were configured for deadlocks and blocking to maintain performance.
High availability technologies such as log shipping, AlwaysOn, mirroring, and replication were implemented and troubleshooted, with knowledge of replication possessed.
Database migrations were successfully completed from one server to another, and DR drills were performed quarterly.
PowerShell scripting was utilized to monitor and restart the SQL service, and to receive email alerts for service status.
TNS entries were maintained for Oracle clients as per client requests, and customer interaction occurred for requirements gathering.
Customers were contacted to provide updates on issue handling and service request resolution at each stage, with proper escalation and notification matrices managed for all support levels.

The individual possessed skills in operating systems like Microsoft Windows Server (2003, 2008, 2008 R2, 2012), database management systems including SQL Server, Sybase ASE Server, Oracle, and MongoDB, and various database tools.

Skills also included high availability and DR technologies, scripting and automation tools like PowerShell, monitoring and ticketing systems such as HP Service Manager and SolarWinds DPA, and web servers like IIS 7.0.

Reporting and ETL tools like SSRS and SSIS were utilized, along with other technical skills such as TNS Entries and soft skills like customer interaction, problem escalation, troubleshooting, and performance tuning.

SSRS is a key component in Sara Hassan’s toolkit, a reporting and ETL tool she skillfully wields alongside SSIS within the complex data landscape of BNY Mellon International Operations. Not a standalone entity, but a powerful instrument in her hands, SSRS facilitates the crucial translation of raw data into actionable insights. It’s a silent workhorse, underpinning the reporting needs of a major financial institution. While Sara’s expertise extends to a broad spectrum of database administration – from SQL Server and MongoDB to Oracle & Sybase – SSRS represents her ability to not just *manage* data, but to *communicate* it effectively. It’s a testament to her proficiency in transforming technical complexity into understandable information, a skill honed through practical application in a demanding, on-site role. SSRS isn’t merely a tool she uses; it’s integral to her function as a Database Administrator III, enabling her to deliver value beyond the technical realm and contribute to informed decision-making.

SQL Server is a robust and versatile database management system, a cornerstone of data infrastructure for organizations like BNY Mellon and Hewlett-Packard. It’s a platform demanding skilled custodians – individuals like Sara Hassan, who have dedicated years to its installation, configuration, and meticulous maintenance. More than just storage, SQL Server facilitates critical business functions through reporting services (SSRS), integration services (SSIS), and automated tasks, ensuring data is not only secure but actively *working*. It thrives within the Microsoft Windows Server ecosystem, from older iterations like 2003 and 2008 to more recent versions. While capable of standing alone, SQL Server often exists alongside other database technologies – Oracle, Sybase, and MongoDB – requiring administrators to navigate a diverse landscape of data solutions. Its security is paramount, demanding regular audits and diligent user management. Ultimately, SQL Server isn’t simply a system; it’s a vital component in disaster recovery strategies and the smooth operation of complex financial and technological infrastructures, a testament to its reliability and power.

Sara Hassan is a highly skilled and experienced Database Administrator, currently a key player at BNY Mellon International Operations in Mumbai. A graduate of PDACOE, Gulbarga, where she earned her B.Tech in Computer Science (2010-2014), Sara quickly established herself in the field, beginning her career in 2014 with Hewlett-Packard India Sales (through Softenger India PVT. LTD) in Bengaluru. There, she honed her foundational skills in SQL Server administration, reporting services, user management, and security. 

Since 2018, she’s advanced to a Database Administrator III role at BNY Mellon, taking on broader responsibilities encompassing SQL Server, MongoDB, Oracle, and Sybase administration. She’s adept at the full lifecycle of database management – from installation and configuration to meticulous maintenance, user access control, complex migrations, and critical disaster recovery planning. Sara isn’t simply a maintainer; she actively leverages tools like SSRS and SSIS to automate tasks and optimize database performance. 

Beyond her professional experience, Sara maintains an active online presence, showcasing her work and connecting with the wider tech community through her GitHub profile, LinkedIn, and personal website, sarahassan.com. She can be reached directly at sara.hassan@email.com or +20 101 234 5678.

SSIS is a powerful, yet often unseen, force within BNY Mellon’s data infrastructure. A key component of Sara Hassan’s toolkit as a Database Administrator III, it’s the engine driving the flow of information – the silent architect of reporting and ETL processes. Not a standalone entity, but a vital skill and tool, SSIS thrives in the hands of those like Hassan who can harness its capabilities for automated database tasks. It’s a workhorse, enabling the seamless migration and manipulation of data, ensuring reports are generated and insights are delivered. While it doesn’t command the spotlight, SSIS is fundamentally linked to the smooth operation of BNY Mellon’s data-driven functions, a crucial element in transforming raw information into actionable intelligence. Its strength lies not in independent action, but in its collaborative role, expertly wielded alongside tools like SSRS and a deep understanding of database administration.

Oracle is a powerful, established database management system, a cornerstone of data infrastructure relied upon by organizations globally. It’s a mature technology, capable of handling complex data needs and demanding workloads. While newer database solutions have emerged, Oracle maintains a significant presence, particularly within larger enterprises. Sara Hassan, a Database Administrator III at BNY Mellon, demonstrates this continued relevance – she’s skilled in its administration alongside more contemporary systems like MongoDB, suggesting Oracle remains a vital component of their operational landscape. It’s a system demanding expertise, a skill set Hassan clearly possesses, alongside proficiency in SQL Server, Sybase, and a range of operating systems, positioning Oracle as a key element within a broader database ecosystem. It’s not merely a database; it’s a legacy system, a workhorse, and a testament to enduring technological value.

India is a land of burgeoning opportunity, a vibrant backdrop for ambition and technological growth. It’s a nation woven with a complex tapestry of cities, each a node in a rapidly expanding network of innovation. Within its borders lie Gulbarga, a city nurturing future technologists; Mumbai, a financial powerhouse; and Bengaluru, a hub for the tech industry. 

It’s in this India that Sara Hassan began her journey, receiving a foundational education in Computer Science at PDACOE in Gulbarga between 2010 and 2014. This grounding propelled her towards a career dedicated to the intricate world of data. 

Since 2018, she has been a key player at BNY Mellon International Operations in Mumbai, expertly managing critical database systems – SQL Server, MongoDB, Oracle, and Sybase – ensuring the smooth operation of financial infrastructure. Prior to that, from 2014 to 2018, she honed her skills at Hewlett-Packard India Sales in Bengaluru (through Softenger India PVT. LTD), mastering SQL Server installation, security protocols, and user management. 

India, therefore, isn’t merely a location in Sara Hassan’s story; it’s the fertile ground where her skills were cultivated and where she now applies them, contributing to the nation’s growing prominence in the global technological landscape. It’s a country that provides both the educational foundation and the professional avenues for individuals like Sara to thrive.

MongoDB is a versatile and powerful database management system, a key component in the modern data landscape. Not a rigid, table-driven relational database, it’s a document database – think flexible, JSON-like documents holding all the information for a given entity. This makes it particularly adept at handling unstructured or semi-structured data, and scaling to meet the demands of rapidly evolving applications. 

While comfortable alongside established players like SQL Server, Oracle, and Sybase – as demonstrated by Database Administrator Sara Hassan’s expertise at BNY Mellon – MongoDB distinguishes itself with its agility and developer-friendliness. Hassan’s role highlights its practical application in a large financial institution, suggesting its reliability and suitability for complex, real-world data management. It’s a skill sought after in database administration, a testament to its growing importance. MongoDB isn’t simply *a* database; it’s a foundational element for building dynamic, scalable applications, offering a different, often more efficient, approach to storing and accessing information.

TNS Entries are the quiet backbone of connectivity for Oracle clients, meticulously configured and maintained on demand. They aren’t glamorous, but essential – the digital address books ensuring seamless communication with Oracle databases. Their existence is born of direct client need; each entry a response to a specific request, shaped by conversations and requirements gathered through dedicated customer interaction. 

Those who work with TNS Entries aren’t simply technicians, but translators – bridging the gap between user need and database access. Proficiency demands not just technical skill in their creation and upkeep, but a practiced hand with reporting and ETL tools like SSRS and SSIS, allowing for monitoring and optimization. More importantly, it requires a knack for problem-solving. Troubleshooting connection issues, escalating complex problems, and fine-tuning performance are all in a day’s work. They are a foundational skill, often paired with a broader technical skillset, a building block upon which more complex database interactions are built. Ultimately, a well-maintained TNS Entry is an invisible success, a silent promise of reliable access kept between client and database.

High Availability isn’t a single thing, but a state of robust readiness – a system’s unwavering promise to remain operational even when faced with disruption. It’s a guardian against downtime, built through layers of redundancy and swift recovery mechanisms. Think of it as a meticulously crafted safety net, woven from technologies like Log Shipping, the always-vigilant AlwaysOn Availability Groups, the mirroring precision of database mirroring, and the distributed resilience of Replication. 

This isn’t merely about *having* these tools, but understanding their intricacies – knowing how to implement them, diagnose their failings, and keep them functioning in harmony. It’s a skillset deeply intertwined with Disaster Recovery, a crucial component in ensuring business continuity.  A practitioner of High Availability speaks the language of PowerShell, automating failovers and monitoring health with practiced ease. They’re fluent in the alerts of systems like HP Service Manager and SolarWinds DPA, interpreting signals to proactively address potential issues before they impact users.  And, often, they’re intimately familiar with the web servers – like IIS 7.0 – that rely on this constant uptime to deliver their services.  High Availability is the silent architect of a seamless user experience, a commitment to keeping the digital world running, uninterrupted.
